cache_door.text: A cellar door set into the earth behind a settlement longhouse is padlocked and marked with a crude painted leaf. A wiry elder sits in a folding chair nearby, watching you with flat grey eyes. She doesn't speak first.

inside_cache.text: The cellar holds rows of sealed clay jars and wax-stopped tins, each labeled in cramped handwriting. The elder explains one wall holds grain and dried legumes; the other holds what she calls the long game — seeds that haven't been planted in decades. She lets you take from the first wall.

choices.label: Help catalog and preserve the tins.

deep_cache.text: Hours of careful work reveal a sealed metal box behind a false panel — old pre-war medical storage repurposed for the rarest seeds. The elder opens it in silence and presses a packet of medicine and a fuel cell into your hands. Payment, she says, for not stealing what you could have taken.

refused_entry.text: The elder doesn't answer. She just stares until you take a step back. No words, no violence — just a wall you can't climb.

medicine_rejected.text: She takes the medicine, examines it, and hands it back. Expired, she says. You get nothing for the offer.

mold_exposure.text: A burst tin releases a cloud of grey spores. Your eyes water and your throat burns for the rest of the afternoon.
